reforms.description: The Italian government introduced tax incentives aimed at stimulating labor demand by granting hiring premiums to firms that employ young people, the unemployed, or disadvantaged individuals, while also fostering entrepreneurship through tax incentives for firms established by young entrepreneurs and disadvantaged individuals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These incentives are designed to stimulate labor demand and encourage entrepreneurship, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms provide significant support for employment and entrepreneurship but do not represent a structural overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The granting of tax breaks to firms which hire young people, unemployed or disadvantaged persons... is available until the end of 1996.

reforms.description: Italy's public support for R&D in business enterprises has significantly increased, contributing to R&D growth, particularly in state-owned enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for R&D is expected to enhance innovation capabilities and productivity in the business sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The increase in public support has been realized and is currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in public support is significant but does not constitute a major reform of the overall R&D funding landscape.
reforms.source_quote: State-owned enterprises contributed more than private firms to R&D growth in the last decade, with public support for R&D in business enterprises significantly increasing.

reforms.description: The lack of institutions specialized in the financing of venture capital has hindered the ability of small- and medium-sized firms to innovate and start new lines of business.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The absence of specialized venture capital institutions limits the capacity of SMEs to innovate, which can negatively impact overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The issue has been recognized and is currently affecting the innovation landscape.
reforms.importance_rationale: Addressing the venture capital gap is significant for fostering innovation but does not represent a comprehensive reform.
reforms.source_quote: The lack of institutions specialized in the financing of venture capital may have affected the ability of small- and medium-sized firms to start up new lines of business.
